parameters.content:
For each file with missing or low coverage:
  1. Fetch source file from GitHub
  2. Build prompt with file context
  3. Generate Jest tests via GPT-4o  ← AI Node (MS1)
  4. Format and enforce author header  ← Code Node (MS1)
  5. Commit test file to new branch
  6. Create Pull Request for review
  7. Trigger coverage workflow on new branch
  8. Wait and fetch new coverage results
  9. Check test results and remove failed tests
  10. Compare old vs new coverage

Credential required: add 'OpenAI Bearer Token' in n8n Settings → Credentials
(Type: HTTP Bearer Auth, value: your OpenAI API key starting with sk-)

parameters.body: {{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o", max_tokens: 4096, temperature: 0.2, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.userPrompt }] }) }}

## Scheduled Production Workflow

Runs automatically every hour.
Prepares handoff payload for downstream pipeline.

parameters.content:
## AI Anti-Pattern Detection

Triggered in **parallel** after PR creation.

1. Build prompt from generated test file content
2. GPT-4o (JSON mode) analyzes for:
   - MISSING_ASSERTIONS: test blocks with no expect()
   - OVERUSE_OF_MOCKS: mocking the layer under test
   - HARDCODED_VALUES: magic literals needing parameterization
   - NO_EDGE_CASES: happy-path only, missing boundary/error tests
   - BRITTLE_SELECTORS: assertions on fragile error strings
   - MISSING_CLEANUP: shared state not reset in afterEach
   - DUPLICATE_SETUP: repeated inline setup instead of beforeEach
3. Returns score 0-100 + structured issue list with suggestions
4. Posts formatted report as GitHub PR comment
5. Sends Telegram alert with score summary

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o", max_tokens: 2048, temperature: 0.1, response_format: { type: "json_object" }, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.userPrompt }] }) }}

parameters.content:
## AI Root Cause Analysis

Triggered in **parallel** when tests fail (Check for Failures = true).

1. Build prompt from: failed step info + generated test file code
2. GPT-4o (JSON mode) classifies failure type:
   - IMPLEMENTATION_BUG: source code bug that tests legitimately expose
   - TEST_BUG: generated test has wrong assertions or setup
   - ENVIRONMENT_ISSUE: missing env var, config, or dependency
   - FLAKY_TEST: non-deterministic (race conditions, timing)
   - IMPORT_ERROR: wrong module path or ESM/CJS mismatch
3. Returns: root cause + confidence + suggested fix + flaky indicators
4. Posts structured analysis as GitHub PR comment
5. Sends Telegram alert with failure summary

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o", max_tokens: 2048, temperature: 0.1, response_format: { type: "json_object" }, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.userPrompt }] }) }}

parameters.content:
## AI Mutation Testing Suggestions

Triggered in **parallel** after tests pass (Log Test Success).

1. Build prompt from: source file (Build Prompt P3) + passing test suite (Format Test File P3)
2. GPT-4o (JSON mode) identifies 6-8 targeted mutations:
   - BOUNDARY: > to >= or < to <=
   - ARITHMETIC: + to - or * to /
   - LOGICAL: && to || (condition flip)
   - NULL_RETURN: return value to return null
   - CONDITION_FLIP: negate boolean condition
   - OFF_BY_ONE: +/-1 on a count or index
   - REMOVE_CALL: remove a function/method call
3. Classifies each: killed (tests catch it) / survived / uncertain
4. Estimates overall mutation score 0-100%
5. Posts suggestions + score as GitHub PR comment
6. Sends Telegram alert with mutation score

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o", max_tokens: 2048, temperature: 0.15, response_format: { type: "json_object" }, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.userPrompt }] }) }}

parameters.content:
## AI Test Classification

Triggered in **parallel** after PR is created.

1. Reads generated test file from Format Test File (P3)
2. Reads source file from Build Prompt (P3)
3. GPT-4o (JSON mode) classifies the generated tests:
   - Type: UNIT | INTEGRATION | UI | NFR
   - Layers tested (controller, model, route, etc.)
   - Scenario coverage (what is covered vs. missing)
   - Quality indicators (assertions, setup/teardown, mocking)
4. Posts structured classification as GitHub PR comment
5. Sends formal Telegram notification to team

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o", max_tokens: 2048, temperature: 0.1, response_format: { type: "json_object" }, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.userPrompt }] }) }}

parameters.content:
## AI Parameterised Test Generation

Triggered in **parallel** after PR is created.

1. Reads source code from Build Prompt (P3)
2. Reads generated test file from Format Test File (P3)
3. GPT-4o (JSON mode) performs BVA + equivalence partitioning:
   - Identifies all input parameters and their valid ranges
   - Generates boundary values (min, max, min-1, max+1, nominal)
   - Generates equivalence partitions (valid/invalid)
   - Produces ready-to-paste Jest `test.each()` blocks
4. Posts structured report as GitHub PR comment
5. Sends formal Telegram notification to team

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "gpt-4o", max_tokens: 3000, temperature: 0.1, response_format: { type: "json_object" }, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.userPrompt }] }) }}

parameters.content:
## AI Reporting + Auto-Fix Workflow (Workflow 6)
1. Aggregate All Test Results
   ↓ Collect data from all test pipelines (unit, integration, UI, NFR)
   ↓ Calculate overall health score and status
   
2. AI Comprehensive Report Generation (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Call OpenAI API with aggregated test data
   ↓ Generate detailed markdown report with 7 sections
   
3. AI Auto-Fix Agent (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Call OpenAI API to analyze test failures
   ↓ Generate corrected test code with confidence scores
   
4. Parse OpenAI Responses
   ↓ Extract report content and auto-fix suggestions
   ↓ Structure data for notifications
   
5. Create Telegram Summary Message
   ↓ Format quick overview with key metrics
   ↓ Include pass/fail stats and AI findings
   
6. Send to Telegram → Summary
   ↓ Quick notification with health score
   
7. Split Report into Chunks
   ↓ Break detailed report into <4000 char segments
   
8. Send to Telegram → Detailed Report (Multiple Messages)
   ↓ Full AI analysis in multiple parts
   
9. Generate HTML Dashboard
   ↓ Create interactive HTML report with charts
   
10. Send to Telegram → HTML Summary
    ↓ Notification about HTML report availability

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "temperature": 0.3,
  "max_tokens": 4000,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a comprehensive test reporting AI agent. Generate a detailed, well-structured test report covering:

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
   - Overall test health score
   - Key findings
   - Critical issues requiring attention

2. UNIT TEST REPORT
   - Pass/fail statistics
   - Anti-patterns detected
   - Coverage analysis
   - Flaky test identification

3. INTEGRATION TEST REPORT
   - API contract compliance
   - Service dependency health
   - Data consistency results

4. UI TEST REPORT
   - Cross-browser results
   - Accessibility compliance
   - Visual regression results
   - User flow coverage

5. NFR TEST REPORT
   - Load test results with SLA compliance
   - Stress test results with breaking points
   - Spike test recovery analysis
   - Performance trends

6. AI-GENERATED RECOMMENDATIONS
   - Test improvements (prioritized)
   - New tests to add
   - Anti-patterns to fix
   - Performance optimizations

7. AUTO-FIX SUGGESTIONS
   - Tests that can be automatically corrected
   - Generated fix code

Format as Markdown for GitHub PR comment.
Use tables, charts references, and clear formatting."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Generate comprehensive test report from:
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}"
    }
  ]
}

parameters.content:
## AI Auto-Commit Test Fixes
1. Prepare Auto-Fix Commit
   ↓ Filter high-confidence fixes (>85%)
   ↓ Prepare commit data and branch name
   
2. Should Auto-Commit?
   ↓ Check if action === 'commit'
   
3. Get Base Branch SHA (HTTP GET)
   ↓ Fetch SHA of main/base branch
   
4. Extract SHA
   ↓ Parse response and get commit SHA
   
5. Create New Branch (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Create new branch from base SHA
   
6. Prepare File Commits
   ↓ Loop through files, base64 encode content
   
7. Commit Files to Branch (HTTP PUT - batched)
   ↓ Update each file on the new branch
   
8. Aggregate Commits
   ↓ Collect all commit responses
   
9. Create Pull Request (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Create PR from new branch to base
   
10. Parse PR Response
    ↓ Extract PR URL and number
    
11. Notify via Telegram
    ↓ Send notification with PR link

parameters.content:
## NFR Test AI Agent Pipeline (Load/Stress/Spike/Endurance)
1. NFR Test AI Agent (HTTP)
   ↓ POST to OpenAI API to analyze NFR test scripts
   ↓ Uses original prompt about k6/JMeter/Gatling analysis
   
2. Parse NFR AI Response
   ↓ Extract JSON from OpenAI response
   ↓ Structure analysis data
   
3. Generate NFR Test Scripts
   ↓ Create k6 scripts for load/stress/spike/endurance tests
   ↓ Use AI-suggested configurations
   
4. Execute Load Test (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Trigger load test on CI server
   
5. Execute Stress Test (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Trigger stress test on CI server
   
6. Execute Spike Test (HTTP POST)
   ↓ Trigger spike test on CI server
   
7. Aggregate NFR Test Results
   ↓ Combine results from all 3 test types
   
8. AI NFR Performance Analysis (HTTP)
   ↓ POST to OpenAI API to analyze test results
   ↓ Uses original prompt about performance engineering
   
9. Parse Performance Analysis
   ↓ Extract analysis from OpenAI response
   ↓ Structure recommendations and findings
   
10. Notify NFR Results via Telegram
    ↓ Send summary to Telegram

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a performance/NFR testing expert AI agent. Your responsibilities:

1. ANALYZE existing NFR test scripts (k6, JMeter, Gatling, Artillery)
2. DETECT issues:
   - Unrealistic load profiles
   - Missing think times
   - Incorrect ramp-up patterns
   - Missing SLA/threshold definitions
   - Insufficient metrics collection
   - Missing correlation for dynamic data

3. GENERATE optimized test configurations for:
   - LOAD TEST: Steady-state performance under expected load
   - STRESS TEST: Find breaking point with gradually increasing load
   - SPIKE TEST: Sudden burst of traffic
   - ENDURANCE/SOAK TEST: Extended duration for memory leaks
   - BREAKPOINT TEST: Find maximum capacity

4. DEFINE proper thresholds and SLAs

Return JSON:
{
  "analysis": {"issues": [{"type": string, "description": string, "fix": string}]},
  "loadTestConfig": {"script": string, "vus": number, "duration": string, "thresholds": object},
  "stressTestConfig": {"script": string, "stages": array, "thresholds": object},
  "spikeTestConfig": {"script": string, "stages": array, "thresholds": object},
  "enduranceTestConfig": {"script": string, "vus": number, "duration": string, "thresholds": object},
  "breakpointTestConfig": {"script": string, "stages": array},
  "customMetrics": [{"name": string, "type": string, "description": string}],
  "slaDefinitions": [{"metric": string, "threshold": string, "rationale": string}]
}"
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Analyze these NFR test files and generate optimized configurations:

Files: {{ JSON.stringify($json.classification.nfrTests) }}

Code:
```
{{ $json.testCode }}
```"
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.3,
  "max_tokens": 4000
}

={

1. PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS:

2. BOTTLENECK IDENTIFICATION:

parameters.content:
## Integration Test Generation Workflow
1. Webhook Trigger          → Receive source files & repo info
2. Prepare Input            → Extract repo, branch, source files
3. AI Generator (HTTP)      → OpenAI generates integration tests
4. Parse Tests              → Extract + validate generated tests
5. AI Anti-Pattern Check    → OpenAI checks for test smells
6. Parse Quality Report     → Extract quality score & gate
7. Quality Gate?            → PASSED → commit | FAILED → notify
8. Prepare Commit Files     → Base64 encode test files
9. Get Branch SHA           → Fetch current branch SHA
10. Split Files             → One item per file
11. Commit Tests            → PUT each file to GitHub
12. Telegram Notification   → Success or failure message

parameters.jsCode:
// Build Telegram success notification
const data = $('Parse Anti-Pattern Report').first().json;

const message = [
  '✅ *Integration Tests Generated & Committed*',
  '',
  `📦 *Repository:* ${data.repository}`,
  `🌿 *Branch:* ${data.branch}`,
  '',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  '📊 *Generation Summary*',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  `• Total Generated: ${data.totalGenerated}`,
  `• Valid Tests: ${data.validTests}`,
  `• Files Committed: ${data.totalFilesToCommit || 0}`,
  '',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  '🔍 *Quality Analysis*',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  `• Quality Score: ${data.qualityScore}/100`,
  `• Quality Gate: ✅ ${data.qualityGate}`,
  `• Issues Found: ${data.issueCount}`,
  `• Critical Issues: ${data.criticalIssues}`,
  '',
  `🕐 *Timestamp:* ${data.timestamp}`
].join('\n');

return [{ json: { message, data } }];

parameters.content:
## NFR Test Generation Workflow
1. Webhook Trigger          → Receive API endpoints & repo info
2. Prepare Input            → Extract repo, branch, base URL, endpoints
3. AI Generator (HTTP)      → OpenAI generates all 4 k6 scripts
4. Parse Scripts            → Extract + validate all scripts
5. AI Quality Check         → OpenAI checks for NFR-specific issues
6. Parse Quality Report     → Extract quality score & gate
7. Quality Gate?            → PASSED → commit | FAILED → notify
8. Prepare Commit Files     → Base64 encode all 4 k6 scripts
9. Get Branch SHA           → Fetch current branch SHA
10. Split Files             → One item per script
11. Commit Scripts          → PUT each k6 file to GitHub
12. Telegram Notification   → Success or failure with script status

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "temperature": 0.3,
  "max_tokens": 4000,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a performance testing expert. Generate k6 test scripts for FOUR scenarios.

1. LOAD TEST:
   - Ramp up to 100 VUs over 2 minutes
   - Hold at 100 VUs for 5 minutes
   - Ramp down over 1 minute
   - Thresholds: p95 < 500ms, error rate < 1%

2. STRESS TEST:
   - Ramp from 0 to 200 VUs over 5 minutes
   - Continue ramping to 500 VUs over 5 minutes
   - Hold at 500 VUs for 3 minutes
   - Ramp down over 2 minutes
   - Find the breaking point

3. SPIKE TEST:
   - Normal load: 50 VUs for 1 minute
   - Spike to 500 VUs instantly
   - Hold spike for 30 seconds
   - Drop back to 50 VUs
   - Repeat 3 times

4. SOAK TEST:
   - Ramp to 100 VUs over 2 minutes
   - Hold steady for 30 minutes
   - Monitor for memory leaks and degradation

For EACH script include:
- Proper checks() for response validation
- Custom metrics (Rate, Trend, Counter, Gauge)
- Tags for grouping
- Think time between requests
- handleSummary() for custom reporting
- SLA threshold definitions

Return JSON:
{
  "loadTest": {
    "fileName": string,
    "filePath": string,
    "script": string,
    "description": string,
    "expectedDuration": string,
    "thresholds": object
  },
  "stressTest": {
    "fileName": string,
    "filePath": string,
    "script": string,
    "description": string,
    "expectedDuration": string,
    "thresholds": object
  },
  "spikeTest": {
    "fileName": string,
    "filePath": string,
    "script": string,
    "description": string,
    "expectedDuration": string,
    "thresholds": object
  },
  "soakTest": {
    "fileName": string,
    "filePath": string,
    "script": string,
    "description": string,
    "expectedDuration": string,
    "thresholds": object
  },
  "summary": {
    "totalScripts": number,
    "baseUrl": string,
    "endpointsCovered": [string],
    "notes": string
  }
}"
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Generate k6 NFR test scripts for the following:

Repository: {{ $json.repository }}
Base URL: {{ $json.baseUrl }}

API Endpoints to Test:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.apiEndpoints) }}

Existing NFR Code (for reference):
```
{{ $json.existingNfrCode }}
```

Generate all 4 NFR test scripts (load, stress, spike, soak)."
    }
  ]
}

={
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "temperature": 0.2,
  "max_tokens": 2000,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a performance testing expert. Review the following k6 NFR test scripts and detect issues:

1. UNREALISTIC_LOAD_PROFILE: Load pattern does not reflect real-world usage
2. MISSING_THINK_TIME: No sleep() between requests causing unrealistic load
3. MISSING_THRESHOLDS: No SLA thresholds defined
4. MISSING_CHECKS: No response validation checks
5. MISSING_CUSTOM_METRICS: No custom metrics for business transactions
6. HARDCODED_BASE_URL: Base URL hardcoded instead of using environment variables
7. MISSING_CORRELATION: Dynamic data not correlated between requests
8. INSUFFICIENT_RAMPUP: Ramp-up too aggressive for realistic testing
9. MISSING_HANDLE_SUMMARY: No custom summary reporting
10. MISSING_TAGS: Requests not tagged for grouping

Return JSON:
{
  "issues": [
    {
      "script": "load|stress|spike|soak",
      "pattern": string,
      "severity": "CRITICAL|WARNING|INFO",
      "description": string,
      "suggestion": string
    }
  ],
  "qualityScore": number,
  "qualityGate": "PASSED|FAILED",
  "overallAssessment": string
}"
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Review these NFR test scripts for issues:

Load Test:
```javascript
{{ $json.scripts.loadTest.script }}
```

Stress Test:
```javascript
{{ $json.scripts.stressTest.script }}
```

Spike Test:
```javascript
{{ $json.scripts.spikeTest.script }}
```

Soak Test:
```javascript
{{ $json.scripts.soakTest.script }}
```"
    }
  ]
}

parameters.jsCode:
// Build Telegram success notification for NFR
const data = $('Parse NFR Quality Report').first().json;
const validations = data.validations || {};

const scriptStatus = (key) =>
  validations[key]?.valid ? '✅' : '❌';

const message = [
  '✅ *NFR Test Scripts Generated & Committed*',
  '',
  `📦 *Repository:* ${data.repository}`,
  `🌿 *Branch:* ${data.branch}`,
  '',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  '📊 *Scripts Generated*',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  `${scriptStatus('loadTest')} Load Test — k6_load_test.js`,
  `${scriptStatus('stressTest')} Stress Test — k6_stress_test.js`,
  `${scriptStatus('spikeTest')} Spike Test — k6_spike_test.js`,
  `${scriptStatus('soakTest')} Soak Test — k6_soak_test.js`,
  '',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  '🔍 *Quality Analysis*',
  '━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━',
  `• Quality Score: ${data.qualityScore}/100`,
  `• Quality Gate: ✅ ${data.qualityGate}`,
  `• Issues Found: ${data.issueCount}`,
  `• Critical Issues: ${data.criticalIssues}`,
  '',
  `🕐 *Timestamp:* ${data.timestamp}`
].join('\n');

return [{ json: { message } }];

main.node: Commit Test File to Separate Branch
